title: Scammers impersonate police and create fake ReportCyber reports to steal cryptocurrency. Victims receive calls from fake AFP officers citing data breaches, then request seed phrases or transfers.
ACMA and National Anti-Scam Centre warn of mobile fraud where criminals take control of phone numbers or make unauthorised account changes to access banking and verification codes.
Investment bonds scam impersonating real businesses offering fake environmental bonds with returns up to 9%. Recent case involved fake websites impersonating a major retailer.
ASIC and ACCC warn of fake crypto trading platforms promoted via messaging apps and social media. Scammers join share trading groups, show fake profits, then request release fees.
Bank impersonation scams increasingly sophisticated with scammers replicating hold music and conducting tag-team calls. One victim lost $350,000 in crypto in 18 hours.
ASIC removed 11,964 phishing and investment scam websites in 2025, averaging 32 per day. AI-powered scams using deepfakes of celebrities are surging on social media platforms.
Employment scams targeting job seekers via social media offering easy remote work. Criminals impersonate recruiters, request upfront payments for training, then steal identities.
ATO and ASIC warn of SMSF early access scams via social media and cold calls. Promoters charge large fees to help people illegally access retirement savings before eligibility.
AI voice cloning scams allow criminals to impersonate family members in emergency calls after recording just seconds of conversation from unknown callers asking basic questions.
Scammers exploit social media age ban confusion by posing as platforms requesting ID verification or offering fake age-verified accounts. No legitimate platform requests ID via text.
Australians lost $2.18 billion to scams in 2025, with investment scams accounting for $837.7 million. Losses increased 7.8% despite stabilized report volumes, showing higher effectiveness.
Victorian man sentenced to over 2 years for bulk phone porting scam targeting victims' mobile numbers to access banking and cryptocurrency accounts through identity theft.
